member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.ToAllAsync(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,System.Func{NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{NxGraph.Result}}[])
member.summary:
Starts the graph with a node that runs all concurrently (each started with the node's own , all awaited) and joins on the combined result: Success iff every work returned Success, else Failure. This is the recommended shape for overlapping I/O — parallel regions and token fork/join interleave cooperatively and never overlap in time.
No early abort: a failing work does not cancel its siblings — all works run to completion before the results combine, so partial effects are never timing-dependent and a per-node .Retry(...) re-runs all works. A throwing work propagates its exception after all works settle (first exception, WhenAll semantics); cancelling the machine's token cancels all works.
Disjoint-keys contract: the works share one routed , which is not thread-safe — concurrent works must touch disjoint keys. Same-key access from two works is a data race; distinct keys write to distinct slots and are genuinely safe. The recommended shape is one Graph-scoped output port per work, combined by the next node. An empty or null works array throws at wiring time, as does a null entry; a single work is legal (it degenerates to ToAsync).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.ToAll(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,System.Func{NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,NxGraph.Result}[])
member.summary:
Starts the graph with the sync twin of : all run sequentially, in order, within one tick, and the results combine identically — Success iff every work returned Success, else Failure. Wall-clock overlap is an async-only mechanic; the join semantics are the same, including no early abort: all works always run, even after an early Failure, so a graph moved between runtimes sees the same board effects. The node is plain and also runs under the async machine via the sync-logic adapter.
The disjoint-keys contract of the async twin is not load-bearing here (works never overlap), but keeping each work on its own keys makes the graph portable to ToAllAsync. An empty or null works array throws at wiring time, as does a null entry; a single work is legal.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.WithSchema(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardSchema)
member.summary: Declares a blackboard schema on the graph being built, routed by the schema's scope: Graph-scoped schemas become the graph's own declaration, Global-scoped schemas record the global board the graph requires, and Node-scoped schemas declare transient per-visit scratch that each machine auto-creates its own board for. Machines validate bound boards against these declarations at WithBlackboard(...) time.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.WithBlackboard``1(``0,NxGraph.Blackboards.Blackboard)
member.summary: Binds a blackboard to the machine, routed into the scope slot its schema declares. Call once per scope; rebinding a scope replaces the board. Chainable in any order with WithAgent: graph.ToAsyncStateMachine<Enemy>().WithBlackboard(worldBb).WithBlackboard(enemyBb).WithAgent(enemy).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.SubGraph(NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken,NxGraph.Graphs.Graph,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a child graph as a composite state and wires a transition to it. The child runs to completion within the parent's step. With enabled, a failed child resumes at its last-active node when the parent re-enters the composite (see ); without it, re-entry restarts the child.

member.summary: Starts the graph with a child graph as its first (composite) state.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.SubGraph(NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken,NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelStepMode,NxGraph.Graphs.Graph,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a sync child-graph composite and wires a transition to it — the runtime-parity twin of . Without history the child runs as a nested sync ; with a failed child resumes at its last-active node on re-entry (see ). decides whether the child completes within one tick (, usable from both runtimes) or advances one node per tick (, sync runtime only).

member.summary: Starts the graph with a sync child-graph composite as its first state (see ).

member.summary: Adds an orthogonal-regions composite and wires a transition to it: every region graph progresses one node per round (cooperative interleaving) until all reach a terminal result. Succeeds only when every region succeeded (see ).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.Parallel(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,NxGraph.Graphs.Graph[])
member.summary: Starts the graph with an orthogonal-regions composite as its first state.

member.summary: Adds a sync orthogonal-regions composite and wires a transition to it — the runtime-parity twin of the async overload (see ). decides whether the join completes within one tick () or spreads one round per tick across frames (, sync runtime only).

member.summary: Starts the graph with a sync orthogonal-regions composite as its first state (see ).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.To``1(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardKey{``0},System.Func{NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,``0})
member.summary:
Starts the graph with a port-producing step: the lambda computes a value from the machine-bound routed blackboard context, the relay writes it to and returns Success. A port is an ordinary Graph-scoped — one key per producing step, named for the datum, not the step (several steps may legally produce the same port). Declare its schema on the graph via WithSchema(...) and bind a board per machine via WithBlackboard(...), so one graph template serves N machines with distinct port values.
Producer steps cannot fail — the relay always succeeds after the write. A step that can fail keeps using To(Func<BlackboardContext, Result>) with an explicit bb.Set(...) on its success path; exceptions propagate as from any relay. Node-scoped keys are rejected at wiring time (their value resets before the consumer runs). Global-scoped keys are allowed but shared across machines — two machines over one template overwrite each other's values, so prefer Graph scope.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.To``1(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardKey{``0},System.Func{``0,NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,NxGraph.Result})
member.summary: Starts the graph with a port-consuming step: the relay reads and hands the value (plus the routed context) to the lambda, returning its result. A consumer that can run before any producer sees the key's registered default — register a meaningful default or guard in the step. Node-scoped keys are rejected at wiring time; Global-scoped keys are allowed but shared across machines — prefer Graph scope.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.Dsl.To``2(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardKey{``0},NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardKey{``1},System.Func{``0,NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,``1})
member.summary: Starts the graph with a port-piping step: the relay reads , hands the value (plus the routed context) to the lambda, writes the transformed value to , and returns Success. Pipe steps cannot fail — a step that can keeps using To(Func<BlackboardContext, Result>) with explicit Get/Set. Node-scoped keys are rejected at wiring time; Global-scoped keys are allowed but shared across machines — prefer Graph scope.

member.summary: Chains a port-producing step (see ).

member.summary:
Adds a brand-new timed async node and wires a transition to it. Example:
GraphBuilder.Start() .ToWithTimeoutAsync(2.Seconds(), _ => ResultHelpers.Success) .ToAsync(_ => ResultHelpers.Success) .ToAsyncStateMachine();

member.summary: Wraps the next async state logic in a timeout and starts the graph with it.
member.param: The start token, which is the entry point of the FSM graph.
The duration of the timeout for the next state logic.
The logic for the next state, which will be wrapped in a timeout.
The behavior to apply when the timeout occurs.
member.returns: A new state token that represents the next state logic wrapped in a timeout.

member.summary: Adds a brand-new timed sync node and wires a transition to it — the runtime-parity twin of . The deadline is checked between ticks (see ): a node that returns past the timeout produces the timeout outcome per .

member.summary: Returned by ForkTo(...). The fork's chain is closed — branches continue inside their lambdas — so the only remaining operations are graph-level.

member.summary: The fork node's id (useful with -style helpers).

member.summary: Finishes the DSL and produces an immutable .

member.name: T:NxGraph.Authoring.TokenDsl
member.summary: Authoring surface for the token runtime (spec 007): fork fan-out and graph→machine factories. Join convergence needs no dedicated DSL — construct one and route any number of chains to it with .To(join) (nodes dedupe by logic reference).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.TokenDsl.ForkTo(NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken,System.Func{NxGraph.Authoring.ForkBranch,NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken}[])
member.summary: Wires this state to a : when a token completes this state, it continues into the first branch and one new token spawns per remaining branch. Each lambda builds one branch chain; converge branches by routing them to the same instance. Token machines only — the FSM runtimes throw on fork nodes.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.TokenDsl.ForkTo(NxGraph.Authoring.StartToken,System.Func{NxGraph.Authoring.ForkBranch,NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken}[])
member.summary: Starts the graph with a fork: the root token fans out immediately at run start.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Authoring.GraphBuilder
member.summary: A builder class for constructing a finite state machine graph.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.GraphBuilder.WithSchema(NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardSchema)
member.summary: Declares a blackboard schema on the graph, routed by the schema's scope: a Graph-scoped schema becomes the graph's own declaration, a Global-scoped schema records the global board the graph requires. Declaring the same scope twice throws — declaration is one-time authoring intent (runtime rebinding is a machine concern).

member.summary: Add the failure transition from to : when the node returns Failure, the machine routes there instead of terminating.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.GraphBuilder.AddGoto(NxGraph.Graphs.NodeId,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a transition from to the node whose display name is . The name is resolved at Build(), so it may be assigned later in the chain. Unknown or ambiguous names fail the build.

member.summary: Add a sync-only node. Wraps it in a .

member.exception: Thrown if no start node has been added or if there are inconsistencies in the graph structure.

member.summary: Returned by the DSL whenever you add a state. It lets you fluently wire transitions and continue building without exposing NodeId.

member.summary: Adds a transition from the current state to .

member.summary: Adds a brand-new async state and immediately wires a transition to it.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.To(NxGraph.Graphs.ILogic)
member.summary: Adds a brand-new sync state and immediately wires a transition to it.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.WithOutcome(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Declares the terminal outcome reported when a run ends at this state (e.g. "Approved" vs "Rejected"), with an optional display name for the code.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.WithUid(System.Guid)
M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.OnEnter(System.Action)
member.summary: Attaches an action fired when the machine enters this state — once per visit, before its first execution (retries do not re-fire it).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.OnExit(System.Action)
member.summary: Attaches an action fired when the machine leaves this state — once per visit, after its final execution, regardless of outcome.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.Retry(System.Byte,System.TimeSpan,NxGraph.Fsm.BackoffKind)
member.summary: Declares a retry policy for this state: on Failure it is re-run in place until it succeeds or executions are consumed, then normal failure handling applies. Backoff is honored by the async runtime only.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.OnError(NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken)
member.summary: Routes this state's Failure outcome to instead of terminating the machine. The success chain continues from this state.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.OnError(NxGraph.Graphs.ILogic)
member.summary: Adds a brand-new sync state as the failure handler for this state. The handler is terminal unless wired further; the success chain continues from this state.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Authoring.StateToken.OnErrorAsync(NxGraph.Graphs.IAsyncLogic)

Batteries-included typed-key context store. Construction allocates all slot storage up front; /// are zero-allocation and zero-boxing thereafter — including first use.
Owned by a single runner at a time (not thread-safe), like the state machines.
Anti-pattern: do not reach for Dictionary<string, object> as a context — that costs string hashing plus boxing on every access. A slot is a schema-checked array read instead.

member.summary:
The per-machine set of scope-bound boards, stamped onto nodes as one unit. / route by the key's schema scope in O(1) — no fall-through chain, no allocation.
A default-constructed context is valid and empty: any key access throws a precise "no board bound for scope" error, so consumers never need their own null guards.

Reusable, immutable-after-freeze layout of typed slots. Register all keys up front (typically in static initializers); the schema freezes when the first is constructed from it. One schema can back any number of independent blackboards — the sanctioned "one graph template, N entities" pattern.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Diagnostics.Export.MermaidGraphExporter
member.summary: Exports a (single static transition per node) to Mermaid flowchart syntax. Dynamic branches chosen at runtime by IDirector are not emitted (no static edges), but statically-known director targets appear as dashed edges. Token fork/join nodes render first-class: subroutine-bar shapes, solid fork-labeled branch edges (every branch runs — an AND-split, not a choice), the join's in its label, and no terminal edge from forks (a fork never ends a token).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Diagnostics.Validations.GraphValidator.CountStaticInbound(NxGraph.Graphs.Graph,System.Int32)
member.summary: Counts statically-known edges into : success and failure destinations plus director/fork static targets. Dynamic director selections are invisible here, which is why the unsatisfiable-join lint is a Warning, not an Error.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.AllState
member.summary:
Synchronous counterpart of : runs N independent works sequentially, in declaration order, within one call and joins on all of them — Success iff every work returned Success, else Failure. Wall-clock overlap is an async-only mechanic (like retry backoff); the essence — N works, one node, all-succeed join — is identical, so a graph moved between runtimes sees the same board effects.
All works always run, even after an early Failure — the same no-early-abort semantics as the async twin — and a per-node .Retry(...) re-runs all works. Each work receives the machine-bound routed ; the disjoint-keys contract of the async twin is not load-bearing here (works never overlap) but keeping it makes the graph portable to .ToAllAsync. Allocation-free per tick: the works array is construction-time state. Being , this node also runs under the async machine via the sync-logic adapter.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.AllState.#ctor(System.Func{NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,NxGraph.Result}[])
member.param: The works to run in order. At least one is required (a single work degenerates to an ordinary relay); null entries are rejected.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncAllState
member.summary:
Runs N independent async works truly concurrently inside one node and joins on all of them: every work is started with the node's own , all are awaited ( over the materialized tasks), and the results combine afterwards — Success iff every work returned Success, else Failure. This is the wall-clock concurrency primitive: regions structure logic, nodes structure time.
A failing work does not cancel its siblings — all works run to completion and the result is combined afterwards, so partial effects are never timing-dependent and a per-node .Retry(...) re-runs all works. A throwing work propagates its exception out of the node after all works settle (first exception, WhenAll semantics); cancelling the machine's token cancels all works. Each work receives the machine-bound routed : concurrent works must touch disjoint keys — same-key access from two works is a data race, while distinct keys write to distinct slots and are genuinely safe.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncAllState.#ctor(System.Func{NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{NxGraph.Result}}[])
member.param: The works to run concurrently. At least one is required (a single work degenerates to an ordinary async relay); null entries are rejected.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncAllState.RunWork(System.Func{NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{NxGraph.Result}},NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Materializes one work as a task so that a synchronously-throwing work faults its task instead of aborting the start loop — every sibling still starts, and the exception propagates only after all works settle.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncChoiceState
member.summary: Async director that picks between two destinations via a predicate. The blackboard-context overload receives the machine-bound routed context (see ), so branching can read shared memory instead of closing over ad-hoc state.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncCompositeState
member.summary:
A composite state that contains a single child node. Surfaces the child's sub-graphs via and forwards the blackboard context, so agent and blackboard stamping reach wrapped machines instead of stopping at this wrapper.
Deep suspend cannot capture an arbitrary child generically, so this base contributes nothing to a and re-enters fresh after a deep resume. Subclasses (and custom containers) that hold durable position opt in by implementing — the third leg of the container contract beside and forwarding.
member.param: The child node to execute.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncCompositeState.#ctor(NxGraph.Graphs.IAsyncLogic)
member.summary:
A composite state that contains a single child node. Surfaces the child's sub-graphs via and forwards the blackboard context, so agent and blackboard stamping reach wrapped machines instead of stopping at this wrapper.
Deep suspend cannot capture an arbitrary child generically, so this base contributes nothing to a and re-enters fresh after a deep resume. Subclasses (and custom containers) that hold durable position opt in by implementing — the third leg of the container contract beside and forwarding.
member.param: The child node to execute.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncCompositeState.Child
member.summary: The wrapped child logic. Exposed to subclasses so a user container codec can read back what the primary constructor captured (e.g. to serialize a wrapped machine's graph or a logic-codec key for a non-graph child).

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncDynamicParallelState
member.summary:
Dynamic (some-of-many) variant of : at composite entry a selector reads the machine-bound and returns a deciding which region graphs run this execution. Only the selected regions are round-robin stepped (one node per region per round) until all of them reach a terminal result — unselected regions are never stepped and never reset. Join semantics match the static composite: only when every selected region succeeded, else through the parent's unified fault model.
The selector runs once per execution, at entry — the selected set is fixed for the run (a region that must stop early should terminate via its own graph logic). An empty mask is a vacuous join: the composite returns without stepping anything. Mask bits at or above the region count fail loudly at entry. Selectors run once per composite execution, so they must stay allocation-free (compose masks with and |, or precompute them at setup).
Implements because the selector needs the context; the stamping walk stops at settable nodes, so this composite forwards the received context to every region machine itself. Agent stamping still reaches regions via .
Deliberately does not implement : like , every execution evaluates the selector, runs the selected regions to terminal inside one ExecuteAsync, and keeps no durable visit or cross-visit state at a parent step boundary — there is nothing for a deep snapshot to capture, and its absence from one is correct, not a gap.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncDynamicParallelState.Regions
member.summary: The region machines (selected or not).

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncDynamicParallelState.Selector
member.summary: The region selector this composite was constructed with. Exposed (like and the ctor arguments) so serializers can map the delegate back to a registered key — delegates themselves cannot ride a wire payload.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncHistoryState
member.summary:
Wraps a child graph as a composite node with history: when the child fails (or is cancelled) and the parent later re-enters the composite — e.g. after a failure edge and a Goto back — execution resumes at the child's last-active node instead of restarting from its start node. A child that completed restarts from the top on re-entry.
This is shallow history for the wrapped graph: the resumed node itself restarts fresh. Deep history falls out of composition — wrap the nested subgraphs inside the child with their own history composites and each level resumes its own position.
The happy path steps the child at 0 B; the resume bookkeeping allocates a small snapshot only on the failure-recovery path, which is off the hot loop by definition.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncHistoryState.Child
member.summary: The wrapped child machine.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncParallelState
member.summary:
Orthogonal (AND-state) regions via cooperative interleaving: each region is a child graph, and one execution of this composite round-robin steps every still-running region — one node per region per round — until all regions reach a terminal result (the join). The composite returns only when every region succeeded; if any region failed it returns after the join, which then flows through the parent's unified fault model (failure edges, retries).
This is deliberately not thread-concurrent. True parallel execution would require multiple in-flight tasks and a join (WhenAll), which allocate — that variant is out of scope to preserve the library's zero-allocation hot-path guarantee. Interleaved progress gives AND-state semantics at 0 B per step, which covers the game/agent use cases this library targets; run truly CPU-parallel work inside a single node instead.
Deliberately does not implement : every execution runs all regions to terminal inside one ExecuteAsync and resets its bookkeeping on entry, so the composite holds no durable visit or cross-visit state at a parent step boundary — there is nothing for a deep snapshot to capture, and its absence from one is correct, not a gap.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncParallelState.Regions
member.summary: The region machines.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncPortProducerRelayState`1
member.summary: Port-producing relay: runs the step with the machine-bound routed context, writes its value to the output port (an ordinary ), and returns Success. The step cannot fail — a step that can must use the plain context relay with an explicit Set. Steps completing synchronously keep the hot path allocation-free.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncPortConsumerRelayState`1

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncPortPipeRelayState`2
member.summary: Port-piping relay: reads the input port, transforms, writes the output port, and returns Success. The step cannot fail — a step that can must use the plain context relay with an explicit Set.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine`1.SetAgent(`0)
member.summary: Binds the agent (typed context) to this machine. It is applied to the graph's nodes immediately and re-applied at the start of every execution, so several machines can share one with distinct contexts as long as their executions do not overlap. For concurrently-running machines, give each its own graph instance.

member.summary: Non-generic AsyncStateMachine.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.ApplyExecutionContext
member.summary: Hook for derived machines to (re)apply per-machine execution context (e.g. the typed agent) to the graph's nodes. Runs under the execute gate, right before Starting. The base implementation re-stamps the bound blackboard context.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.SetBlackboard(NxGraph.Blackboards.Blackboard)
member.summary: Binds a blackboard into the scope slot its schema declares (replace semantics — like SetAgent, rebinding swaps the board, which enables machine pooling). Applied to the graph's nodes immediately and re-applied at the start of every execution. Validated against the graph's schema declarations when present.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.NxGraph#Blackboards#IBlackboardSettable#SetBlackboards(NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext@)
member.summary: Receives the whole context from a parent machine's stamping walk (nested composite path). Validates against this machine's own graph declarations, so a conflicting child schema fails loudly at stamp time. The parent's Node slot is dropped — Node boards are per-machine scratch; this machine composes its own from its own graph.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.SetRestartPolicy(NxGraph.Fsm.RestartPolicy)
member.summary:
Controls how the machine behaves after reaching a terminal status.
: automatically resets to Ready after completion/failure/cancellation.
: requires explicit ; re-execution throws.
: ignores further execution attempts and returns the terminal result until is called.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.SetAutoReset(System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Backwards-compatible switch for the legacy auto-reset behaviour.
Maps to (true) and (false).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.Reset(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reset to the initial node. Disallowed while Running/Transitioning. Status moves: (any non-running) -> Resetting -> Ready.
member.remarks: Public entry point acquires so an external caller cannot race a running machine. Internal auto-restart paths must call directly because the gate is already held by the surrounding // frame.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.RepairTransientStatus
member.summary: If an observer threw while the machine was in a transient status (Starting/Resetting/ Transitioning), restore Ready without notifications so the machine stays usable — Suspend/Reset reject transient statuses and a redundant re-transition would trip the debug assert in .

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.Suspend
member.summary: Captures the machine's runtime position into a serializable snapshot. Legal only at a step boundary: between calls of a stepped run, or on a machine that is not currently executing. Suspending never touches the run loop.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.Resume(NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineSnapshot)
member.summary: Restores a snapshot produced by onto this machine. The graph must be structurally equivalent to the one the snapshot was taken from (same node indices). A mid-run snapshot is continued with until it returns a terminal result; re-attach the user context via SetAgent before stepping if the graph uses one. No observer events are replayed by the restore itself.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.SuspendDeep
member.summary: Deep variant of : captures this machine's position plus the internal state of every composite in its graph that holds durable state (nested machine positions, history's remembered child position, sync RoundPerTick mid-visit bookkeeping) into a . Same gates as ; the extra cost is one linear walk over the graph's nodes plus the composites' own recursive captures — cold path by contract, allocation is expected. The shallow pair and are untouched; use them when flows keep suspension points at the top level.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.ResumeDeep(NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineDeepSnapshot)
member.summary: Restores a snapshot produced by onto this machine: first the machine's own position via (all its gates and checks apply unchanged), then each captured composite via after validating that the claimed node exists and implements the interface. A composite node absent from re-enters fresh (sparse capture). Node-scoped scratch resumes as defaults at every nesting level.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.StepAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Advances the machine by exactly one node execution, mirroring the sync machine's frame-stepped Execute(). Returns while more nodes remain and the terminal result when the run finishes. The first call begins a run (applying the restart policy exactly like ExecuteAsync); between steps the machine stays . Observer events and terminal status transitions are identical to a full ExecuteAsync run.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.StepCoreAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Advances the machine by exactly one node execution. Returns while more nodes remain (including in-place retries and failure-edge reroutes) and a terminal / when the run finished.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine.TransitionTo(NxGraph.Fsm.ExecutionStatus)
member.summary: Centralized status transition. Sets the status unconditionally and then kicks machine-lifecycle notifications outside the lock.
member.remarks: Uses (unconditional) rather than CAS. Only call this when the caller owns the execution context (i.e. from the run-loop or enter/exit methods that are guarded by ). For external transitions that must validate the predecessor state, use instead.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.DynamicParallelState
member.summary:
Sync twin of (runtime parity): at the start of each visit a selector reads the machine-bound and returns a deciding which region graphs run. Only selected regions are stepped; unselected regions are never stepped (a region left terminal by an earlier visit is reset on the next visit's first tick, selected or not, so stale results never leak across visits). Join semantics match : only when every selected region succeeded, else through the parent's unified fault model.
maps rounds onto ticks exactly as in — evaluates the selector on the visit's first tick only; the selected set is fixed until the join. An empty mask is a vacuous join (immediate ); mask bits at or above the region count fail loudly. Selectors run once per visit — keep them allocation-free (compose with and |, or precompute masks at setup).

member.summary: The region machines (selected or not).

member.summary: How region rounds map onto calls.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.DynamicParallelState.Selector
member.summary: The region selector this composite was constructed with. Exposed (like and ) so serializers can map the delegate back to a registered key — delegates themselves cannot ride a wire payload.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.HistoryState
member.summary:
Synchronous counterpart of : wraps a child graph as a composite node with history. When the child fails (or is cancelled) and the parent later re-enters the composite — e.g. after a failure edge and a Goto back — execution resumes at the child's last-active node instead of restarting from its start node. A child that completed restarts from the top on re-entry.
This is shallow history for the wrapped graph: the resumed node itself restarts fresh. Deep history falls out of composition — wrap the nested subgraphs inside the child with their own history composites and each level resumes its own position.
decides how child nodes map onto ticks: completes the child run inside one call (usable from both runtimes via the sync-logic adapter); advances one child node per call and returns in between (sync runtime only — the async loop rejects node-level ). A parent Suspend() between RoundPerTick ticks does not capture composite-internal position (flat snapshots are primitives-only); resuming that on a fresh graph restarts the visit. Use the parent's SuspendDeep()/ResumeDeep(...) to carry the mid-visit position and the remembered history across a durable boundary (see ).

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.HistoryState.Child
member.summary: The wrapped child machine.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.HistoryState.Mode
member.summary: How child nodes map onto calls.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.ISuspendableComposite
member.summary:
Deep-suspend capture contract for composite node logic. SuspendDeep() on / walks the machine's own graph once and collects a from every logic node that implements this interface; ResumeDeep(...) hands each captured entry back. The machine walk never recurses — a composite recurses by calling SuspendDeep() / ResumeDeep(...) on its own child machine(s), exactly how the blackboard stamping walk stops at settable nodes and lets machines re-walk their own graphs.
The rule for implementing it: a composite implements iff it persists visit or cross-visit state across Execute/ExecuteAsync calls (history's remembered child position, a sync RoundPerTick composite's mid-visit bookkeeping, a nested sync machine mid-run between parent ticks). Composites that run their children to terminal inside one execution and keep no durable fields — the async parallel composites — must not implement it: absence of capture is correct, not a gap.
Container contract. This is the third leg of the contract for custom container nodes, beside (so the agent walk reaches child graphs) and forwarding (so the stamped context reaches child machines): containers that hold durable position should also implement so deep suspend can capture it. A container that does not implement it contributes nothing to the deep snapshot and re-enters fresh after a resume — today's shallow behavior.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.ISuspendableComposite.SuspendComposite(System.Int32)
member.summary: Captures this composite's internal state into a whose is (the owner node's index in the capturing machine's graph). Child/region machines are captured via their own SuspendDeep(), in region order. Cold path — allocation is expected.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.ISuspendableComposite.ResumeComposite(NxGraph.Fsm.CompositeSnapshot)
member.summary: Restores a snapshot produced by onto this composite. Implementations must validate the entry's shape (child snapshot count, done-bit count) against their own structure — throwing naming on mismatch — and deep-resume their child machines, which re-run the full resume validation recursively. Derivable bookkeeping (remaining region count, failure aggregation) is recomputed here, never trusted from the wire.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelState
member.summary:
Synchronous counterpart of : orthogonal (AND-state) regions via cooperative interleaving. Each region is a child graph run by its own sync ; a round advances every still-running region by one node. Rounds repeat until all regions reach a terminal result (the join). The composite returns only when every region succeeded; if any region failed it returns after the join, which then flows through the parent's unified fault model (failure edges, retries).
decides how rounds map onto ticks: completes the whole join inside one call; runs one round per call and returns in between, so region progress aligns 1:1 with game-loop frames. RoundPerTick is only valid under the sync (the async runtime rejects node-level ); RunToJoin works under both runtimes via the sync-logic adapter.
Like the async composite, this is deliberately not thread-concurrent (see the scope decision on ); region graphs must contain sync () node logic, as with any sync-run graph.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelState.Regions
member.summary: The region machines.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelState.Mode
member.summary: How region rounds map onto calls.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelStepMode
member.summary: How a sync composite spreads its child work across calls (ticks). Shared by / (a round = one node per still-running region), and a nested 's own (a round = one node of the child machine).

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelStepMode.RunToJoin
member.summary: Loop rounds inside a single Execute() call until the composite reaches a terminal result — the whole join costs one tick (the caller opts into the work; a child node that keeps returning , e.g. a multi-tick wait, busy-spins for the duration). Mirrors the async composites, which always run to the join, so RunToJoin composites are executable from both runtimes via the sync-logic adapter.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelStepMode.RoundPerTick
member.summary: Advance one round per call, then return ("re-run me next tick"). Rounds align 1:1 with game-loop frames; the terminal result is returned on the tick the composite finishes. Only meaningful under the sync — the async runtime rejects node-level .

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.PortProducerRelayState`1
member.summary: Synchronous counterpart of : runs the step with the machine-bound routed context, writes its value to the output port (an ordinary ), and returns Success. The step cannot fail — a step that can must use the plain context relay with an explicit Set.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.PortConsumerRelayState`1
member.summary: Synchronous counterpart of : reads the input port and hands the value (plus the routed context) to the step, returning its result.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.PortPipeRelayState`2
member.summary: Synchronous counterpart of : reads the input port, transforms, writes the output port, and returns Success. The step cannot fail — a step that can must use the plain context relay with an explicit Set.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.RegionMask
member.summary:
Allocation-free set of region indices for the dynamic parallel composites ( / ), backed by a single — which caps dynamic composites at 64 regions.
Build masks with at setup time (its params array allocates), or compose them allocation-free inside a selector with and : RegionMask.Bit(0) | RegionMask.Bit(2). Selectors run at composite entry — once per composite execution — so they must stay allocation-free to preserve the 0 B guarantee.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.RestartPolicy
member.remarks:
This policy governs behaviour on subsequent Execute()/ExecuteAsync() calls once the machine is terminal:
: automatically resets the machine to .
: requires an explicit reset; re-execution throws until reset.
: re-execution is silently ignored and the last terminal result is returned until reset.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.RestartPolicy.Auto
member.summary: Automatically reset to after the machine completes/fails/cancels.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.RestartPolicy.Manual
member.summary: Stay terminal after completion/failure/cancellation. The caller must explicitly reset the machine. Re-execution without reset is considered an error.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.RestartPolicy.Ignore
member.summary: Stay terminal after completion/failure/cancellation. Subsequent execution attempts are no-ops: the machine does not run any nodes and simply returns the last terminal result until reset.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.BackoffKind
member.summary: How the delay between retry attempts grows.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.BackoffKind.Fixed
member.summary: Every retry waits the same base delay.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.BackoffKind.Linear
member.summary: Retry N waits N × base delay.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.BackoffKind.Exponential
member.summary: Retry N waits 2^(N-1) × base delay.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.RetryPolicy
member.summary:
Per-node retry policy: when the node's logic returns Failure, the machine re-runs it in place until it succeeds or executions have been consumed, then falls through to the normal failure handling (failure edge or terminal failure). Attempt counters live in the executing state machine, not the graph.
Backoff delays are honored by the async runtime only. The synchronous runtime is frame-stepped (one node per Execute() call) and must never block, so it retries on the next tick regardless of the configured backoff.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.RetryPolicy.MaxAttempts
member.summary: Total executions allowed for the node, including the first one. 0 = no policy.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.RetryPolicy.Backoff
member.summary: Base delay between attempts; retries immediately.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.RetryPolicy.None
member.summary: No retry policy — a failing node fails on its first attempt.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.RetryPolicy.DelayForAttempt(System.Int32)
member.summary: The delay to wait before the next attempt, given how many attempts have already failed.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine
member.summary:
Synchronous, zero-allocation state machine that inherits so it can be nested as a node inside a parent graph — mirroring the → relationship.
All threading overhead has been removed:
_status is a plain field (no /).
_executeGate is a plain .
_autoReset is a plain .
No , no async/await, no ValueTask.
Observer callbacks are void (see ).
Stepped execution — call the inherited once per frame (e.g. from Unity's Update method): Result r = fsm.Execute(); // Continue while running, Success/Failure when done Each call advances exactly one node. Nested instances used as nodes are also stepped one level at a time. To run to completion in a non-Unity context, loop until the result is not Continue.
Node logic is executed via — every node in the graph must have its populated (i.e. implement ).

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.LastOutcome
member.summary: The outcome code of the node the last run terminated at (default 0). Reset when a new run starts.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.LastOutcomeName
member.summary: The display name registered for , or null. Resolved lazily — never on the run loop.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.Status
member.summary: Public execution status.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.ApplyExecutionContext
member.summary: Hook for derived machines to (re)apply per-machine execution context (e.g. the typed agent) to the graph's nodes. Runs under the execute gate, right before Starting. The base implementation re-stamps the bound blackboard context.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.SetBlackboard(NxGraph.Blackboards.Blackboard)
member.summary: Binds a blackboard into the scope slot its schema declares (replace semantics — like SetAgent, rebinding swaps the board, which enables machine pooling). Applied to the graph's nodes immediately and re-applied at the start of every run. Validated against the graph's schema declarations when present.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.NxGraph#Blackboards#IBlackboardSettable#SetBlackboards(NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext@)
member.summary: Receives the whole context from a parent machine's stamping walk (nested composite path). Validates against this machine's own graph declarations, so a conflicting child schema fails loudly at stamp time. The parent's Node slot is dropped — Node boards are per-machine scratch; this machine composes its own from its own graph.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.SetRestartPolicy(NxGraph.Fsm.RestartPolicy)
member.summary:
Controls how the machine behaves after reaching a terminal status.
: automatically resets to Ready after completion/failure.
: requires explicit ; re-execution throws.
: ignores further execution attempts and returns the terminal result until is called.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.SetResetPolicy(NxGraph.Fsm.RestartPolicy)
member.summary: Renamed alias of . The sync and async machines shipped with two names for the same feature; both now converge on SetRestartPolicy, matching the enum.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.StepMode
member.summary: How maps child work onto ticks when this machine is used directly or nested as a node: (default) advances exactly one node per call; loops to the terminal result inside one call, which also makes the machine executable as a node under the async runtime via the sync-logic adapter.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.SetStepMode(NxGraph.Fsm.ParallelStepMode)
member.summary: Sets . Machine-level runtime configuration — like the restart policy, it is not part of the graph structure and does not serialize.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.SetAutoReset(System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Backwards-compatible switch for the legacy auto-reset behaviour.
Maps to (true) and (false).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.Reset
member.summary: Reset to the initial node. Disallowed while Running/Transitioning. Status moves: (any non-running) → Resetting → Ready.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.Suspend
member.summary: Captures the machine's runtime position into a serializable snapshot. The sync runtime is frame-stepped, so any point between calls is a legal step boundary — including the middle of a run (the machine stays Running between ticks). Only calling from inside node logic is rejected.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.Resume(NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineSnapshot)
member.summary: Restores a snapshot produced by onto this machine. The graph must be structurally equivalent to the one the snapshot was taken from (same node indices). A mid-run snapshot is continued by calling until it returns a terminal result; re-attach the user context via SetAgent before resuming if the graph uses one. No observer events are replayed by the restore itself.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.SuspendDeep
member.summary: Deep variant of : captures this machine's position plus the internal state of every composite in its graph that holds durable state (nested machine positions, history's remembered child position, RoundPerTick mid-visit bookkeeping) into a . Same gates as — legal between any two ticks, mid-run included; the extra cost is one linear walk over the graph's nodes plus the composites' own recursive captures — cold path by contract, allocation is expected. The shallow pair and are untouched; use them when flows keep suspension points at the top level.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.ResumeDeep(NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineDeepSnapshot)
member.summary: Restores a snapshot produced by onto this machine: first the machine's own position via (all its gates and checks apply unchanged), then each captured composite via after validating that the claimed node exists and implements the interface. A composite node absent from re-enters fresh (sparse capture). Node-scoped scratch resumes as defaults at every nesting level.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.TickInternal
M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachine.TransitionTo(NxGraph.Fsm.ExecutionStatus)
member.summary: Transitions the machine to a new execution status, invoking observer callbacks if the status changed.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineDeepSnapshot
member.summary:
The deep counterpart of : the machine's own position () plus the internal state of every composite node in its graph that holds durable state (). Produced by SuspendDeep() and consumed by ResumeDeep(...) on or built over an equivalent graph (same node indices, per nesting level). Deep snapshots are interchangeable between the two runtimes as long as the graph's nodes are executable by the target runtime.
Like the shallow snapshot, the records contain only primitives and arrays — no polymorphism — so any serializer (e.g. System.Text.Json) handles them with zero configuration; the library never serializes them itself. Capture is sparse: only composites that persist visit or cross-visit state ( implementors) emit entries, and a composite node absent from simply re-enters fresh on resume. The user context (agent/blackboards) is owned by the caller and must be re-attached after resuming; Node-scoped scratch is transient by definition and resumes as defaults at every nesting level.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineDeepSnapshot.#ctor(NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineSnapshot,NxGraph.Fsm.CompositeSnapshot[])
member.summary:
The deep counterpart of : the machine's own position () plus the internal state of every composite node in its graph that holds durable state (). Produced by SuspendDeep() and consumed by ResumeDeep(...) on or built over an equivalent graph (same node indices, per nesting level). Deep snapshots are interchangeable between the two runtimes as long as the graph's nodes are executable by the target runtime.
Like the shallow snapshot, the records contain only primitives and arrays — no polymorphism — so any serializer (e.g. System.Text.Json) handles them with zero configuration; the library never serializes them itself. Capture is sparse: only composites that persist visit or cross-visit state ( implementors) emit entries, and a composite node absent from simply re-enters fresh on resume. The user context (agent/blackboards) is owned by the caller and must be re-attached after resuming; Node-scoped scratch is transient by definition and resumes as defaults at every nesting level.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.CompositeSnapshot
member.summary: One composite node's captured internals inside a : the owner node's index in the capturing machine's graph, the sync RoundPerTick mid-visit flag (, always false from async composites), the parallel per-region done bits ( — captured, not derived, because a dynamic parallel deselection is done-without-terminal-status; empty for single-child composites), and the child/region machines' own deep snapshots in region order ( — length 1 for subgraph/history composites). Join bookkeeping that is derivable (_remaining, _anyFailed) is deliberately not captured — it is recomputed at resume so the wire shape cannot self-contradict.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.CompositeSnapshot.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean[],NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineDeepSnapshot[])
member.summary: One composite node's captured internals inside a : the owner node's index in the capturing machine's graph, the sync RoundPerTick mid-visit flag (, always false from async composites), the parallel per-region done bits ( — captured, not derived, because a dynamic parallel deselection is done-without-terminal-status; empty for single-child composites), and the child/region machines' own deep snapshots in region order ( — length 1 for subgraph/history composites). Join bookkeeping that is derivable (_remaining, _anyFailed) is deliberately not captured — it is recomputed at resume so the wire shape cannot self-contradict.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.DeepSnapshots
member.summary: Shared deep-suspend plumbing for both FSM machines: the linear capture walk over one graph's nodes, the resume-side validation/dispatch, and the composite-side shape checks. The walk itself never recurses — composites recurse by calling SuspendDeep() / ResumeDeep(...) on their own child machines, mirroring the agent/blackboard stamping walks — so a thread-local depth counter guards the machine-hop recursion with the same limit and error style as Graph.MaxStampingDepth.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.DeepSnapshots.MaxDepth
member.summary: Mirrors Graph.MaxStampingDepth.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.DeepSnapshots.Capture(NxGraph.Graphs.Graph)
member.summary: One linear O(nodes) walk over collecting from every logic node whose async-then-sync logic slot implements the interface (the same probing order as the stamping walks). Cold path by contract — allocates the entry list and arrays.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.DeepSnapshots.ResumeComposites(NxGraph.Graphs.Graph,NxGraph.Fsm.CompositeSnapshot[])
member.summary: Validates every composite entry against (index in range, the claimed node implements ) before dispatching any restore, then hands each entry to its composite. Composite-side shape checks and the recursive child validation run inside .

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.DeepSnapshots.ValidateShape(NxGraph.Fsm.CompositeSnapshot,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Composite-side shape validation: the entry must carry exactly the child snapshots and done bits this composite expects, else the graph is not equivalent to the snapshot's. Errors name the composite's node index.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.DeepSnapshots.ResumeChild(NxGraph.Fsm.Async.AsyncStateMachine,NxGraph.Fsm.CompositeSnapshot,System.Int32)
member.summary: Deep-resumes one child machine, contextualizing any validation error with the owning composite's node index (the child machine's own messages have no parent-graph index).

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.StateMachineSnapshot
member.summary:
A serialization-friendly capture of a state machine's runtime position — current node, status, retry progress, and terminal outcome. Produced by Suspend() and consumed by Resume(...) on or built over an equivalent graph (same node indices — e.g. one round-tripped through NxGraph.Serialization). Snapshots are interchangeable between the two runtimes as long as the graph's nodes are executable by the target runtime.
The snapshot deliberately contains only primitives, so it serializes cleanly with any serializer. The user context (agent/blackboard) is owned by the caller and must be re-attached via SetAgent after resuming.

Snapshots are interchangeable between the two runtimes as long as the graph's nodes are executable by the target runtime.
The snapshot deliberately contains only primitives, so it serializes cleanly with any serializer. The user context (agent/blackboard) is owned by the caller and must be re-attached via SetAgent after resuming.

member.summary: Branches like a switch/case. Finishes immediately with ; the runtime asks for the next node. The blackboard-context overload receives the machine-bound routed context (see ), so the selector can read shared memory instead of closing over ad-hoc state. Purely synchronous — the authoring layer wraps this in a so that async runtimes can also execute it.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.TimeoutBehavior
member.summary: Outcome policy when a timeout occurs.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.TimeoutBehavior.Fail
member.summary: Return from the node.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Fsm.TimeoutBehavior.Throw
member.summary: Throw a .

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.TimeoutState
member.summary:
Synchronous counterpart of : wraps an with a deadline. The first of a visit records a timestamp; each tick runs the inner logic once. A terminal inner result passes through and ends the visit. When the inner logic returns past the deadline, the wrapper produces the timeout outcome per : returns into the unified fault model (failure edges, retries — a retry starts a fresh deadline); throws .
Mechanics differ from the async twin by design: the sync runtime has no cancellation, so the deadline cannot interrupt a node mid-execution — it is detected between ticks, after the inner logic returns. No CTS, no timers, no allocation on the happy path.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.TimeoutState.#ctor(NxGraph.Graphs.ILogic,System.TimeSpan,NxGraph.Fsm.TimeoutBehavior,System.Func{System.Int64})
member.summary: Creates a timeout wrapper over a custom time source. must return monotonic timestamps in units — inject scaled or paused game time, or a fake clock for deterministic tests.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Fsm.WaitState
member.summary:
Synchronous counterpart of : a multi-tick wait for the frame-stepped runtime. The first of a visit records a timestamp; every tick until the duration elapses returns ("re-run me next tick"), then . Zero or negative durations complete immediately. Completing the wait clears the recorded timestamp, so re-entering the state starts a fresh wait.
Sync runtime only: returning node-level across ticks is the sync machine's multi-frame contract; the async loop rejects it (use .WaitForAsync(...) there). No timers, no allocation — the wait is a timestamp comparison per tick.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.WaitState.#ctor(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Creates a state that waits for before succeeding.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Fsm.WaitState.#ctor(System.TimeSpan,System.Func{System.Int64})
member.summary: Creates a wait over a custom time source. must return monotonic timestamps in units — inject scaled or paused game time, or a fake clock for deterministic tests.

Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified nodes and edges.
member.param: The unique identifier for the graph.
The array of nodes in the graph. Must be non-empty and start with the start node at index 0.
The array of transitions (edges) in the graph. Must be non-empty and have the same length as the nodes array.
The logic associated with the graph. If null, an empty logic is used.
Optional per-node stable UIDs indexed by ( = unassigned). Must match the nodes array length when provided.
member.exception: Thrown when the nodes or edges arrays are empty, have unequal lengths, or the first node is not the start node.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.Schema
member.summary: The Graph-scoped declared at authoring time via WithSchema(...), or null when the graph declares none. Declarations are opt-in validation for machine binding; a graph round-tripped through serialization has no declarations and binds permissively.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.GlobalSchema
member.summary: The Global-scoped schema this graph requires, or null when it declares none.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.NodeSchema
member.summary: The Node-scoped (transient per-visit) schema declared via WithSchema(...), or null when the graph declares none. Machines auto-create their own board from this schema — Node boards are never user-bound, shared, or serialized.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.RetryPolicies
member.summary: The per-node retry policies indexed by , or null when no node declares one. Exposed for the runtimes and serializers.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.OutcomeCodes
member.summary: Per-node terminal outcome codes indexed by , or null when no node declares one. When a run terminates at a node, the machine reports the node's code (default 0) as LastOutcome.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.Uids
member.summary: Per-node stable UIDs indexed by , or null when no node has one; marks an unassigned slot. UIDs are identity metadata for external tooling (editors persisting layouts, breakpoints, references across rebuilds) — runtime identity stays the index and no run loop reads them. Uniqueness scope is this graph only; nested subgraphs may reuse uids.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.OutcomeNames
member.summary: Optional display names for outcome codes, resolved lazily for reporting — never on the run loop.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.MaxStampingDepth
member.summary: Cap on composite nesting for the agent/blackboard stamping walks. Indirect graph cycles (A nests a machine over B, B nests one over A) are constructible with public APIs; without a cap the walk would recurse to a process-killing StackOverflowException. Depth-capping instead of a visited set keeps run-start stamping allocation-free.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Graphs.Graph.SetBlackboards(NxGraph.Blackboards.BlackboardContext@)
member.summary: Stamps the routed blackboard context onto every node that implements , recursing into composites via . Unlike this never throws when nothing accepts: the state base classes accept automatically, so zero acceptors carries no bug signal — the lint lives in the graph validator instead.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Result
member.summary:
Represents the outcome of a state-machine operation.
Use the static fields , , and for standard results, or the factory methods / to attach a diagnostic message.
Equality is based on only — two results with the same code but different messages are considered equal. This keeps assertions like Is.EqualTo(Result.Success) working regardless of message.

member.summary:
The operation has not finished yet — it is a status report, not a command.
Returned by the stepped runtimes (, AsyncStateMachine.StepAsync) while more nodes remain, and by sync node logic to indicate the current node needs another tick (e.g. a multi-frame Unity state such as a timer or animation wait).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Tokens.AsyncTokenMachine`1.SetAgent(`0)
member.summary: Binds the agent (typed context) to this machine. It is applied to the graph's nodes immediately and re-applied at the start of every execution, so several machines can share one with distinct contexts as long as their executions do not overlap.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.AsyncTokenMachine
member.summary:
The asynchronous token runtime — the twin with the async FSM's mechanics: ValueTask<Result>, cancellation awareness, atomic status transitions, and retry backoff honored via (awaited inline during the failing token's turn). N pooled tokens flow through one flat graph via cooperative round scheduling; / nodes are interpreted structurally, and ordinary nodes keep their FSM semantics per token.
ExecuteAsync runs the whole flow; StepAsync advances exactly one scheduling round and returns while tokens remain, mirroring the sync machine's granularity. Semantics (run lifecycle, join firing, starvation, outcome aggregation) are identical to the sync twin; only the mechanics differ, per the runtime-parity convention.

member.summary: Public execution status (volatile for visibility).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Tokens.AsyncTokenMachine.Resume(NxGraph.Tokens.TokenMachineSnapshot)
member.summary: Restores a snapshot produced by Suspend() (from either token runtime) onto this machine. The graph must be structurally equivalent (same node indices) and the pool large enough for the snapshot's live tokens. A mid-run snapshot is continued with until it returns a terminal result. Node-scope scratch is transient and comes back as defaults.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Tokens.AsyncTokenMachine.StepAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Advances the machine by exactly one scheduling round, mirroring the sync machine's frame-stepped Execute(). Returns while tokens remain and the terminal result when the run finishes. The first call begins a run (applying the restart policy exactly like ExecuteAsync); between rounds the machine stays .

member.summary: Lands on node , resolving fork/join structure at arrival — same semantics as the sync twin.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.ForkState
member.summary:
Structural fan-out node for the token runtimes: a token passing through a fork continues to [0] and one new token is spawned per remaining branch. Forks carry no user logic and their slot must stay empty — the branches replace the success edge (the DSL enforces this; the validator lints it).
Fork nodes are interpreted only by / . The FSM runtimes cannot express multiple active nodes, so executing a fork under them throws instead of silently misrouting. The / implementations exist solely to surface the branches to reachability validation and Mermaid export via EnumerateStaticTargets() — selection likewise throws.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Tokens.ForkState.Branches
member.summary: The branch heads, in declaration order. Branch 0 continues the arriving token.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinKind
member.summary: Discriminates how a decides it has enough arrived tokens to fire.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinKind.All
member.summary: Fire when all n expected tokens have arrived (classic AND-join).

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinKind.Any
member.summary: Fire on every arrival — the join is a mid-graph merge point.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinKind.Quorum
member.summary: Fire when m of the expected tokens have arrived (M-of-N quorum).

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinPolicy
member.summary: The firing rule of a . A join accumulates arriving tokens and fires whenever the accumulated count reaches : the requirement is consumed, one token continues along the join's success edge, and the other consumed tokens retire. Joins re-arm — they can fire many times per run, which is what makes a merge point and lets stream-style token flows pass through.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinPolicy.Kind
member.summary: The firing rule discriminator.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinPolicy.Count
member.summary: The declared count (n for All, m for Quorum, 1 for Any).

member.name: P:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinPolicy.RequiredCount
member.summary: Arrivals consumed per firing. A default-constructed policy is invalid (0).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinPolicy.All(System.Int32)
member.summary: Fire when tokens have arrived.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinPolicy.Any
member.summary: Fire on every arrival (mid-graph merge).

member.name: M:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinPolicy.Quorum(System.Int32)
member.summary: Fire when of the incoming tokens have arrived.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinState
member.summary:
Structural merge node for the token runtimes: arriving tokens are parked until the 's requirement is met, then the join fires — the firing token continues along the join's ordinary success edge, the other consumed tokens retire, and the join re-arms for the next batch. makes the join a mid-graph merge point (every arrival passes straight through).
Join nodes are interpreted only by / ; executing one under the FSM runtimes throws. Convergence is authored by routing several chains to the same instance (.To(join) dedupes by reference into one node).

member.name: P:NxGraph.Tokens.JoinState.Policy
member.summary: The firing rule of this join.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.TokenSlotPhase
member.summary: Pool-slot lifecycle of a token. slots are available for spawning; only / tokens exist logically.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.Token
member.summary: A unit of execution state in the token runtimes: its position, per-visit attempt counter, lifecycle phase, and (when the graph declares a Node-scope schema) its own transient scratch board. Tokens are pooled — every slot (board included) is preallocated at machine construction so spawning and retiring are free-list index operations at 0 B.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.Token.SlotIndex
member.summary: The pool slot this token permanently occupies (set once at pool creation).

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.Token.Id
member.summary: Stable identity within a run (dense, ascending from 0).

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.Token.NodeIndex
member.summary: Index of the node this token currently occupies (or is parked at).

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.Token.Attempts
member.summary: Executions of the current node by this token in this visit.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.Token.NodeEntered
member.summary: Whether the current node's EnterAction has fired for this token's visit.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.Token.SpawnRound
member.summary: The scheduling round this token was spawned in. A token never runs in its spawn round — it first executes in the following round, keeping interleaving deterministic.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.Token.NodeBoard
member.summary: Per-token Node-scope scratch, created from the graph's declared Node schema; null when none is declared. Resets exactly when this token's attempt counter resets.

member.summary: A synchronous token machine with an agent, mirroring the way mirrors .

member.name: M:NxGraph.Tokens.TokenMachine`1.SetAgent(`0)
member.summary: Binds the agent (typed context) to this machine. It is applied to the graph's nodes immediately and re-applied at the start of every run, so several machines can share one with distinct contexts as long as their runs do not interleave.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.TokenMachine
member.summary:
The synchronous token runtime: N pooled tokens flow through one flat graph concurrently via cooperative round scheduling — each round advances every runnable token by one node. nodes fan tokens out, nodes merge them (all / any / M-of-N), and every ordinary node keeps its FSM semantics (retry policy, failure edge, enter/exit hooks) applied per token.
This is the third runtime beside the sync/async FSMs — the single-active-node FSM machines are untouched, and a graph without fork/join nodes runs identically under either family. Like the sync , the machine inherits so it can be nested as a node inside a parent graph, steps one scheduling round per call by default (), and drains to the terminal result in one call under . Scheduling is deliberately not thread-concurrent, preserving the 0 B hot-path guarantee.
A run starts with one token at the start node and ends when no runnable token remains: if tokens are still parked at joins the machine fails (join starvation); otherwise it succeeds only when no token died (failed terminally). Machine status stays between rounds — the FSM's per-edge status is a single-active-node concept and is not reported by the token machines.

member.name: F:NxGraph.Tokens.TokenMachine.MaxCascadeDepth
member.summary: Cap on fork/join arrival cascades resolved within a single token step. Fork→fork or join→fork chains are resolved recursively at arrival time; a cycle of forks would otherwise recurse forever while spawning tokens.

member.summary: Public execution status.

member.summary: Captures the machine's runtime state into a serializable snapshot. The sync runtime is frame-stepped, so any point between calls is a legal round boundary — including the middle of a run. Only calling from inside node logic is rejected.

member.summary: Restores a snapshot produced by (from either token runtime) onto this machine. The graph must be structurally equivalent (same node indices) and the pool must be large enough for the snapshot's live tokens. A mid-run snapshot is continued by calling until it returns a terminal result. Node-scope scratch is transient and comes back as defaults.

member.name: T:NxGraph.Tokens.TokenMachineSnapshot
member.summary:
A serialization-friendly capture of a token machine's runtime state: the multiset of live tokens plus per-join arrival counts. Produced by Suspend() and consumed by Resume(...) on a or built over an equivalent graph (same node indices); snapshots are interchangeable between the two token runtimes. This is a separate contract from — the flat FSM snapshot shape is deliberately untouched.
The snapshot contains only primitives and plain records, so it serializes cleanly with any serializer. Node-scope scratch is transient by definition and resumes as defaults; the user context (agent/blackboards) is owned by the caller and must be re-attached after resuming.

member.name: M:NxGraph.Tokens.TokenMachineSnapshot.#ctor(NxGraph.Fsm.ExecutionStatus,System.Boolean,System.Int32,NxGraph.Tokens.TokenRecord[],System.Int32[])
member.summary:
A serialization-friendly capture of a token machine's runtime state: the multiset of live tokens plus per-join arrival counts. Produced by Suspend() and consumed by Resume(...) on a or built over an equivalent graph (same node indices); snapshots are interchangeable between the two token runtimes. This is a separate contract from — the flat FSM snapshot shape is deliberately untouched.
The snapshot contains only primitives and plain records, so it serializes cleanly with any serializer. Node-scope scratch is transient by definition and resumes as defaults; the user context (agent/blackboards) is owned by the caller and must be re-attached after resuming.

member.name: P:NxGraph.Tokens.TokenMachineSnapshot.AnyTokenDied
member.summary: Whether any token had already died (failed terminally) before the snapshot was taken — the resumed run must still report at its natural end.
